claim: Hospital-domain shift causes severe reliability degradation (acc 0.964->0.620, AUROC 0.993->0.770, sensitivity 0.944->0.405)
caveat: Development-stage; capped balanced subsets (id_val n=500, ood_val n=2000); ResNet-18; NOT final performance

claim: Temperature scaling improves calibration metrics on ood_val (ECE 0.233->0.092, Brier 0.276->0.216, NLL 0.915->0.609)
caveat: Fit and evaluated on the same ood_val subset (optimistic); development-stage

claim: Calibrated confidence removes high-confidence false negatives at chosen thresholds in this ood_val subset (>=0.90-conf FNs 222->0)
caveat: Threshold- and subset-specific magnitude effect; does not change decisions; development-stage

claim: Deterministic uncertainty is weaker under OOD than ID (entropy error-detection AUROC 0.688 vs 0.927)
caveat: Deterministic single-forward-pass uncertainty only; development-stage

claim: Temperature scaling is argmax-invariant: confidence magnitude changes but decisions/ranking metrics do not
caveat: Interpretive/mathematical statement; still development-scope data

claim: Confidence-based abstention has a hard ceiling under shift (@50% coverage OOD-cal risk 0.380->0.250; 198/595 FNs retained)
caveat: Development-stage; capped ood_val subset; no operating threshold committed

claim: Selective prediction is near-perfect ID (risk->0.000 at 50% coverage; all 14 FNs abstained)
caveat: ID capped subset (n=500); contrast-only with OOD; development-stage

claim: Reproducible locked-split streaming protocol with a reserved test hospital (methodological contribution)
caveat: Protocol claim, not a performance claim

claim: Findings motivate model-based uncertainty and domain-generalization as next steps
caveat: Motivational framing only; those methods not implemented

claim: Headline OOD generalization performance for an unseen hospital
caveat: Unlocks ONLY after the single locked ood_test (center 2) evaluation under written authorization

claim: Calibration generalizes to OOD (not merely fits this subset)
caveat: Unlocks after a calibration split distinct from the evaluation subset

claim: Model-based uncertainty (MC-dropout / ensemble) improves OOD reliability
caveat: Unlocks only if MC-dropout/ensembles are implemented and run (currently deferred)

claim: Superiority over domain-generalization / uncertainty methods
caveat: Unlocks only after a DG/uncertainty baseline comparison

claim: Committed abstention operating point for deployment
caveat: Unlocks after threshold selection on ood_val; none committed

claim: Clinical deployment / clinical-use readiness
caveat: Patch-level, capped-subset development analysis; no clinical endpoint or WSI aggregation

claim: Final generalization performance of the model/method
caveat: No full-split or held-out test evaluation has been run

claim: Superiority over all/most DG or uncertainty methods
caveat: No baselines exist in this study

claim: Any conclusion about ood_test (center 2)
caveat: ood_test is reserved for a single final evaluation; unread in all milestones

claim: Paper-level final metrics quoted from capped development subsets
caveat: Capped balanced subsets are not representative full-distribution performance

claim: Calibration improves accuracy / sensitivity / decisions
caveat: Temperature scaling is argmax-invariant; decisions and accuracy are unchanged

claim: Abstention makes OOD predictions safe / solves domain shift
caveat: Confident OOD errors are retained, not abstained; risk stays high
